data.task:
Fix the symmetric 409-masking defect in the ticket-launch path in packages/ui/src/renderer/App.tsx. CONTEXT: run_253 already fixed `doLaunch` (App.tsx ~574-608) so that a 409 is only shown as the generic 'A run is already in flight for this workspace.' when the daemon error actually matches in-flight (`res.status === 409 && /in flight/i.test(res.error)`), and ANY OTHER 409 falls through to the `else` branch that surfaces `res.error` verbatim. But `doLaunchTicket` (App.tsx ~613-639) was missed: its 409 handler is `else if (res.status === 409)` and hardcodes 'A run is already in flight for this workspace.' for EVERY 409 — masking real, distinct 409 reasons. The most important masked case is the pending-close refusal in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:794 ('ticket "X" already has run Y awaiting founder close confirmation ...'), which a founder would currently see as the wrong 'already in flight' message.

WHAT TO CHANGE: In `doLaunchTicket`, branch the 409 handler exactly the way `doLaunch` already does — guard the generic 'already in flight' message on `res.status === 409 && /in flight/i.test(res.error)`, and for any other 409 fall through to surfacing the daemon's real `res.error` verbatim (same shape as doLaunch's final `else`: `const message = res.error || `Launch failed (${res.status}).``, setLaunchProgressError + notify('err', ...)). Match the surrounding style and the established doLaunch pattern precisely; do not invent a new pattern.

MUST NOT BREAK: do not touch `doLaunch`, `launchIndependentHandoff`, or any daemon/launcher code — this atom is the ticket-launch UI handler only. Do not change the in-flight behavior (a genuine in-flight 409 must still show the generic message). Preserve all unrelated code.

TESTS: add or extend a test in packages/ui/tests/live-app.test.tsx that asserts a NON-in-flight 409 from the ticket-launch path (e.g. the pending-close refusal error string) is surfaced verbatim to the founder, NOT replaced by 'already in flight'; and keep/confirm a test that a genuine in-flight 409 still shows the generic message.

ACCEPTANCE: (1) launching a fix run for a ticket that returns a non-in-flight 409 shows the daemon's real error verbatim; (2) a genuine in-flight 409 still shows 'A run is already in flight for this workspace.'; (3) `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test` (or the repo's UI test command) passes; (4) typecheck passes. Report the exact diff and the test command output.

data.reason: doLaunchTicket 409 now guards on /in flight/ and surfaces real daemon errors verbatim (mirrors doLaunch); 2 new tests cover both branches; all 171 UI tests pass and tsc --noEmit exits 0.

data.question:
Ticket 0069 scope decision. State of play, verified against the live tree: AC-1 (the headline bug — misleading 'already in flight' 409) is now FULLY fixed: doLaunch was fixed in run_253 and I just had Bob fix the symmetric doLaunchTicket gap (committed a322423, all 171 UI tests + typecheck green). AC-2 (runnerless launch affordance) and the 'flag priorities as runnerless' half of AC-3 ALSO already shipped in run_253: the /runs/independent-handoff endpoint, the durable handoff .md artifact with begin-run instructions, and the 'Handoff'/'Create handoff' button + runnerless badge.

TWO genuine gaps remain before 0069 fully meets its acceptance:
  (1) AC-3's other half — a PENDING handoff is not discoverable before its first run record lands. The daemon emits a 'runnerless-handoff' event and writes the .md artifact, but NO status surface consumes it (verified: zero consumers); the founder only gets a transient toast. Surfacing pending handoffs is net-new daemon+UI work — exactly the slice the ticket itself flagged for its own Objective.
  (2) AC-4 — the exact request-deb-repair / governed-write failure you hit while filing this bug was never captured (command + error), so it can't be triaged.

My recommendation: CLOSE 0069 now on the fix (AC-1 done; AC-2 + runnerless-flagging shipped), and (a) promote the pending-handoff discoverability to its own focused priority per the ticket's own scope-split guidance, and (b) capture gap (2) as a follow-up ticket. After your answer there is concrete next work either way — if you instead want it built inside THIS run, I'll delegate the daemon+UI atom; if you agree to close+promote, I'll file the follow-up(s) and close 0069. Either way, can you paste the exact command and error text from the governed-write/request-deb-repair failure so AC-4 is captured?
